findings.title: Reset tokens use predictable non-CSPRNG generation
Reset token verification does not consume the token

reasoning: bcrypt cost 12 with bcrypt-managed salts and bcrypt.compare is used for passwords. Reset-token comparison is timing-safe and the reset endpoint returns a generic message. However, reset tokens are generated with Date.now and Math.random rather than a CSPRNG, and successful verification never marks the token used, so tokens are reusable until expiry.
